name: Framely
description: Framely: The open-source, lightweight, multi-tenant drag-and-drop website editor for modern web applications, powered by Next.js
readme_summary: Framely is an open-source, full-stack website builder boilerplate built with Next.js. It provides a drag-and-drop interface for creating websites, supports multi-tenancy with subdomains, and includes user authentication via Clerk.
primary_use_case: A boilerplate for developers to quickly build and deploy multi-tenant websites with a drag-and-drop editor.
cover_image_prompt: A master architect standing in a digital workshop, effortlessly assembling a beautiful, modern website from floating blocks and components using a holographic drag-and-drop interface. The architect is placing a 'header' block onto a 'page' canvas, which instantly materializes into a polished web page. Subtle UI elements from Framely, like a component library panel and a site preview window, are visible on floating screens. The scene is bathed in a soft, creative glow, with digital particles representing design elements swirling around. The overall style is a clean, 3D isometric illustration with vibrant colors, emphasizing ease of use and creative power.

name: map-to-poster
description: MapToPoster JS is a client-side web app that turns any location into a high-resolution, customizable map poster with various themes, layouts, and export options.
readme_summary: MapToPoster JS is a client-side JavaScript web application that allows users to generate high-resolution, customizable map posters from any location. It offers a hybrid rendering system, dynamic styling options, and pro-grade export capabilities for creating personalized geographic art.
primary_use_case: Generating custom, high-resolution map posters for wall art or personalized gifts.
cover_image_prompt: A skilled cartographer, depicted as a modern artist, stands in a sunlit studio. In front of them is a large, blank canvas. With a magical stylus, they are drawing a detailed map of a city, which is simultaneously appearing on the canvas as a beautiful, framed poster. The input is a glowing globe showing a selected location, and the output is the finished, high-resolution map poster. Subtle UI elements showing map controls and export options float around the artist. The scene is rendered in a 3D isometric illustration style, emphasizing creativity and precision, with a clean, bright color palette.

name: ui-act
description: A seamless Computer Use agent for Linux
readme_summary: UI Act is a Rust-based computer use agent for Linux that automates tasks by controlling the user's mouse and keyboard. It integrates with the desktop environment, allowing users to continue working while the agent performs actions triggered by text prompts, leveraging Multi-Pointer X for independent control.
key_features: Automates computer tasks via text prompts
Uses its own mouse and keyboard for actions
Works alongside the user without hogging input
Integrates directly into the Linux desktop environment
Distributes as a Debian package for Ubuntu
Provides CLI tool and GNOME extension for interaction
primary_use_case: To automate repetitive or complex GUI-based tasks on a Linux desktop by interpreting natural language prompts and executing them using simulated mouse and keyboard input.
cover_image_prompt: A friendly, stylized robot hand seamlessly taking over a human's mouse and keyboard, which are glowing with digital energy. The robot hand is typing on the keyboard and moving the mouse to interact with a vibrant, simplified desktop interface displaying a flowing stream of text prompts being transformed into completed tasks represented by checkmarks and finished icons. Subtle UI elements and data particles float around, indicating the agent's actions. The background is a clean, modern workspace. The overall mood is helpful and efficient. Style: 3D isometric illustration with a bright, clean color palette and clear, friendly character design.

name: PicView
description: Fast, free and customizable picture viewer
readme_summary: PicView is a fast, free, and customizable image viewer for macOS and Windows. It supports a wide array of image formats, including animated WEBP, GIF, SVG, AVIF, HEIC, and PSD, offering a clean user experience without ads or tracking. Users can choose between light and dark themes and customize the interface.
key_features: Supports numerous image formats (WEBP, GIF, SVG, AVIF, HEIC, PSD, etc.)
primary_use_case: Viewing and managing a wide variety of image file formats on desktop operating systems with a customizable and efficient user interface.
cover_image_prompt: A magical, ornate picture frame stands on a workbench. Inside the frame, a vibrant, dynamic image of a phoenix is transforming from a pile of scattered, diverse image file icons (like JPG, PNG, GIF, WEBP, PSD, SVG) into a single, perfectly rendered digital artwork. A gentle stream of light flows from a small, stylized 'PicView' logo on the workbench towards the frame, illuminating the transformation. The background is a clean, modern studio with subtle UI elements and code snippets faintly visible on a nearby screen. The overall mood is one of effortless creation and organization. Style: 3D isometric illustration with a clean, bright aesthetic and soft, glowing highlights.
